title: 10 possible side effects of Ammi visnaga (Visnaga) if used improperly
intro: Ammi visnaga, also known as Bishop's weed, has been used in traditional medicine for various health conditions. However, if used improperly, it can cause several side effects. First, it can irritate the gastrointestinal tract, resulting in symptoms such as nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. Prolonged use or high doses can lead to photodermatitis, a skin condition characterized by sensitivity to sunlight. Additionally, it may affect blood pressure and heart rate, causing them to drop dangerously low. Moreover, Ammi visnaga can interact with certain medications, including blood thinners and sedatives, leading to adverse effects. Pregnant women should avoid using it as it may stimulate the uterus, leading to miscarriage. Therefore, it is crucial to consult with a healthcare professional before using Ammi visnaga for medicinal purposes.
